item.title: One Year In, SonicWall’s MSS Evolution Is Just Getting Started
Say Goodbye to Clunky VPNs with SonicWall VPNaaS
item.description: Tired of outdated VPNs slowing you down? See how SonicWall Cloud Secure Edge offers safer, simpler remote access that grows with your business.

item.title: Modernize Remote Access with VPNaaS from SonicWall
item.description: Traditional VPNs are slow, risky and outdated. Discover how SonicWall Cloud Secure Edge makes remote access safer, faster and easier to manage.

item.title: What’s New in SonicOS 7.2.0: Smarter Access and Security
item.description: SonicOS 7.2.0 brings smarter authentication, Wi-Fi security, and time syncing to your firewall. See how it helps improve access and control.

item.title: Wing FTP Server Remote Code Execution: CVE-2025-47812
Oracle Identity Manager Authentication Bypass Vulnerability (CVE-2025-61757)
item.description: Oracle Identity Manager Authentication Bypass Vulnerability (CVE-2025-61757)

item.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Under Siege: Active Exploitation of Dual Zero-Days
Understanding SalatStealer: A Threat Actor’s Golang Stealer Toolset
React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) Critical Unauthenticated RCE
Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) OneView Software, Remote Code Execution (CVE-2025-37164)
item.description:
HPE OneView is a software-defined infrastructure management platform that centralizes and automates the monitoring and management of HPE servers, storage, and networking. A potential security vulnerability has been identified in Hewlett Packard Enterprise OneView Software. This vulnerability could be exploited, allowing a remote unauthenticated user to perform remote code execution. A critical vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-37164, has been discovered in HPE OneView, affecting all versions up to and including v10.20. This flaw enables unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on the server, earning a perfect CVSS score of 10.0.
References: CVE-2025-37164

item.title: N8n AI Workflow Automation Remote Code Execution Vulnerability (CVE-2025-68613)
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team became aware of a Critical Remote Code Execution (RCE) Vulnerability in n8n, a flexible AI workflow automation platform, assessed its impact, and developed mitigation measures. N8N is frequently used to automate repetitive operational tasks and to integrate security tools and SaaS platforms. In simple words, using n8n users build workflows composed of nodes, with each node representing an action such as making an API request, processing data, or sending an email.

item.title: Multiple vulnerabilities in SolarWinds Web Help Desk Leading to RCE: CVE-2025-40551
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team became aware of a critical vulnerability chain in SolarWinds Web Help Desk (WHD), assessed its impact and developed mitigation measures. SolarWinds WHD is an IT Ticketing and Asset Management solution designed for small-to-medium IT teams to automate service requests and track hardware/software inventory. The issues, tracked as CVE-2025-40536 (Security Protection Bypass) and CVE-2025-40551 (a deserialization bypass in the AjaxProxy functionality), impact all versions prior to 2026.1. When chained together, these flaws allow unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on the server. Given the widespread use of SolarWinds WHD, organizations are strongly advised to apply the vendor-provided updates as soon as possible.

item.title: GNU inetutils-telnetd remote authentication bypass to RCE (CVE-2026-24061)
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team became aware of a Critical authentication bypass vulnerability in GNU –a Unix-like operating system, assessed their impact, and developed mitigation measures. GNU is a collection of many programs: applications, libraries, developer tools, even games. This critical authentication bypass vulnerability in GNU inetutils-telnetd allows unauthenticated remote attackers to gain instant root shell access via malicious NEW_ENVIRON telnet option exploitation, identified as CVE-2026-24061, affecting versions 1.9.3 up to and including version 2.7. With a CVSS score of 9.8 (Critical) due to its low attack complexity, high impact, and the fact that it affects core application functionality. As described in PoC, the impact of an argument injection can result into Remote Code Execution. The root issue is argument injection through unsafe string interpolation of client-controlled data. Hence, all organizations that use GNU instances are strongly encouraged to update their instances to the latest patched version as a matter of urgency.

item.title: Saturday Night Cyber: How the SonicWall SOC Saved the Day
item.description: See how the SonicSentry SOC detected and stopped a Saturday night cyberattack before it became a major incident.

item.title: OpenClaw Auth Token Theft Leading to RCE: CVE-2026-25253
item.description:
OpenClaw, previously known as Clawdbot and referred to as Moltbot in early versions, is an AI assistant framework that runs locally on macOS, Windows, or Linux. It supports cloud-based models such as Anthropic and OpenAI, as well as local models.
The issue, tracked as CVE-2026-25253, affects all versions prior to 2026.1.29. This flaw allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to steal an authentication token, which can then be used to achieve remote code execution, earning a high CVSS score of 8.8. As OpenClaw continues to gain popularity and broader adoption, the risk associated with this vulnerability increases significantly. Users are strongly encouraged to apply the vendor-provided updates without delay.

item.title: SonicOS 8.2.0: Better Security Outcomes, Zero Extra Cost
Stop Comparing Features. Start Demanding Outcomes
item.description: Buying more security tools doesn’t guarantee protection. See why outcomes depend on integration, management and continuous delivery.

item.title: SonicWall Earns 5-Star Rating in 2026 CRN Partner Guide
item.description: SonicWall’s SecureFirst Partner Program earns a 5-Star rating in the 2026 CRN Partner Program Guide, recognizing exceptional partner enablement.

item.title: SonicWall Named Leader in 2026 GigaOm Firewall Radar
item.description: SonicWall is named a Leader and Fast Mover in the 2026 GigaOm Radar for Enterprise Firewalls, highlighting innovation and proven threat protection.

item.title: Michael Crean Discusses Iran Cyber Threats on Fox News
item.description: SonicWall SVP Michael Crean appeared on Fox News to discuss Iran-linked cyber activity and the Stryker attack. Watch the full interview.

item.title: Juniper JunOS OS Evolved Pre-authenticated Remote Code Execution (CVE-2026-21902)
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team became aware of a severe unauthenticated Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource vulnerability in Juniper Networks Junos OS Evolved (PTX Series), assessed their impact, and developed mitigation measures. Juniper PTX Series routers are high-performance core and peering routers built for high throughput, low latency, and scale. They are commonly used by internet service providers, telecommunication services, and cloud network applications.

item.title: Three Decades for a 3-Line Fix: The Critical telnetd Bug Hiding in Plain Sight (CVE-2026-32746)
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team became aware of an out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the Telnet server shipped with GNU Inetutils, assessed its impact and developed mitigation measures. Telnetd hardly needs an introduction. It is one of the oldest and most widely distributed network utilities on Linux systems. The issue, tracked as CVE-2026-32746, affects all telnetd versions up to 2.7. This vulnerability, classified as a classic buffer overflow (CWE-120), allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to crash the server and potentially execute arbitrary commands. It carries a critical CVSS score of 9.8. Contrary to popular belief that Telnet is dead, it remains hidden within the vital infrastructure that runs the world. Hence, users are strongly encouraged to apply the vendor-provided patch or restrict/disable the telnet access without delay.

item.title: n8n Expression Sandbox Bypass RCE - CVE-2026-1470
item.description: Critical CVSS 9.9 RCE vulnerability in n8n AI workflow automation via expression sandbox bypass. SonicWall IPS signatures IPS:21799 and IPS:22086 available.

item.title: Langflow Public Flow Build Endpoint RCE - CVE-2026-33017
item.description: Critical CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated RCE in Langflow AI via build_public_tmp endpoint passes user input to exec() without sandboxing. SonicWall IPS signatures available.

item.title: Automated Cybersecurity Response: Why Detection Isn’t Enough
item.description: Cyber threats move fast. Learn why detection alone isn’t enough and how automated security response helps stop attacks before they spread.

item.title: The Frontier of AI Belongs to Businesses of All Sizes
item.description: SonicWall is ensuring small-to-medium-sized organizations reap the same benefits of frontier AI that enterprises do.

item.title: AI in Cybersecurity: How SonicWall Balances Innovation and Resilience
item.description: Discover how AI is reshaping cybersecurity defense and risk. Learn how SonicWall's RTDMI, DPI, and SAMI help MSPs and enterprises stay secure in an AI-driven threat landscape.

item.title: Android Arsink RAT Revisited Targeting User Credentials
Understanding the MITRE ATT&CK Framework and Evaluations – Part 2
item.description: Capture Client delivers capabilities that are underscored by the ATT&CK framework. Here’s how CISOs can leverage these capabilities to define and implement their security strategy.

item.title: Rethinking Security Architecture with Adversary Frameworks
item.description: Explore how combining the Cybersecurity Kill Chain and MITRE ATT&CK helps build resilient security architecture with SonicWall MXDR.

item.title: The Next Wave in Cybersecurity: Smarter Tools for MSP Growth
item.description: Discover SonicWall’s latest tools for MSPs — powerful, easy-to-use security that scales with your business and cuts through the complexity.

item.title: Smarter Network Management With NSM 3.0 and NSa Firewalls
item.description: Explore how NSM 3.0 and NSa firewalls help businesses and MSSPs simplify operations, strengthen security and lower total cost of ownership.

item.title: Introducing SonicWall Unified Management: Smarter Security for MSPs
item.description: SonicWall launches SonicWall Unified Management to help MSPs streamline operations, reduce complexity, and improve security.

item.title: PARTNER POWER NEWS: Essential Updates for Every SonicWall Partner
item.description: Surf the cybersecurity wave with killer updates to supercharge your MSP business! From next-gen firewalls to hot promos and can’t-miss events, here’s your fast-track guide to what’s new and ready to boost your growth this month!

item.title: Managed Firewalls: The Upgrade You’re Overdue For
item.description: Firewall breaches often come down to human error. Learn how managed firewall services can help reduce risk and ease the burden.

item.title: SonicOS 7.3.3 for GEN7: What MSPs and Channel Partners Need to Know
item.description: SonicOS 7.3.3 delivers Generative AI content filtering, default-on credential auditing, DTLS VPN support, and more for GEN7 appliances. Here is what MSPs and channel partners need to know before upgrading.

item.title: The Death of the Firewall — Why It Isn't Going Anywhere | SonicWall
item.description: The firewall has been declared dead for decades. So why is the market still growing? SonicWall CPO Chandrodaya Prasad explains why it is not going anywhere.

item.title: The Firewall Is Not Dead: How AI Makes It Smarter | SonicWall
item.description: Discover how AI-powered firewalls and SonicWall's Unified Management platform are redefining network security for enterprises and MSPs in 2024 and beyond.

item.title: Cyber Hygiene Fundamentals: Passwords, MFA, and Zero Trust in 2026
item.description: Weak passwords and outdated network access are still the most common entry points for attackers. Here is what good cyber hygiene actually looks like for small businesses in 2026.

item.title: Healthcare Can't Catch a Break From Cyberattacks — And Attackers Know It
item.description: Attack volumes dropped across every major industry in 2026 — except healthcare. Here's why threat actors aren't leaving, and what the data says about how they're getting in.

item.title: Layer 3 vs. Layer 4 vs. Layer 7 Firewalls: Where Do Virtual Firewalls Fit In?
item.description: Understand the differences between Layer 3, Layer 4, and Layer 7 firewalls, and learn where virtual firewalls fit in cloud and hybrid network security architectures.

item.title: Next.js WebSocket Upgrade Handler SSRF - CVE-2026-44578
item.description: High-severity CVSS 8.6 SSRF in self-hosted Next.js servers via crafted WebSocket upgrade requests. SonicWall IPS signatures available.

item.title: NGINX Rewrite Module Heap Overflow RCE - CVE-2026-42945
item.description: Critical CVSS 9.2 heap buffer overflow in the NGINX rewrite module enables unauthenticated remote code execution. SonicWall IPS signatures available.

item.title: Joomla Content Editor Unauthenticated RCE - CVE-2026-48907
item.description: Critical CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated RCE in the Joomla Content Editor (JCE) extension allows PHP web shell upload and code execution. SonicWall IPS signature available.

item.title: SonicWall Cloud Secure Edge Expands PoP Network
item.description: SonicWall Cloud Secure Edge PoP expansion adds five new locations across North America and Europe, delivering faster Zero Trust network access closer to your users.

item.title: How SonicWall CSE Extends Capture ATP to Remote Users
item.description: SonicWall CSE's Secure Internet Access capability brings the same Capture ATP file inspection available on your firewall to every device, regardless of location.

item.title: Stateful vs Stateless Firewall: Key Differences in Virtual Firewalls | SonicWall
item.description: Discover how stateful and stateless firewalls differ in virtual environments. Learn which inspection method best fits your cloud security architecture with SonicWall.

item.title: File Browser Hook OS Command Injection - CVE-2026-35585
item.description: High-severity CVSS 7.2 command injection in File Browser's Command Runner enables authenticated RCE via a crafted filename. SonicWall IPS signature available.

item.title: CVE-2025-7544: Attackers Weaponize Tenda AC1206 Router Vulnerability to Deploy Mirai-Related Malware
item.description: The SonicWall Capture Labs threat research team has identified active exploitation attempts targeting CVE-2025-7544, a critical stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability affecting Tenda AC1206 routers

item.title: SonicWall NSM Configuration Auditor: Audit Your Firewall in Seconds
item.description: Discover how the SonicWall NSM 4.0 Configuration Auditor Report helps IT teams automatically assess firewall configurations, reduce risk, and enforce best practices at scale.
